scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Circular Dependency in Agent Outputs
justification: Two of three circular dependency links identified (Bird↔Pippen, MJ↔Pippen) but the full circular chain (Bird→MJ→Pippen→Bird) is never described as a loop — it's presented as two independent missing_input escalations rather than a connected cycle. The Bird→MJ assumption link is absent. Foundational gap (missing/invalid tenant context handling) is clearly named. Two of three specific unknowns are well-articulated (error response for missing context, async context construction), but 'what constitutes valid tenant context' is only implicitly touched. Escalation is present with actionable options. What IS defined is correctly summarized in high_confidence_areas. No behavior is invented — options are explicitly framed as choices for Bird/MJ to make ('Bird must make this call, not Shaq'). However, the core requirement of identifying the circular dependency pattern is only partially met.

observations.text: Correctly identifies that no agent defined missing/invalid tenant context failure behavior and flags it as security-critical
Correctly identifies the MJ-Pippen gap on async TenantContext construction from job payloads
Does not invent error handling — presents options and explicitly says Bird must decide, not Shaq
High/low confidence separation cleanly distinguishes what is defined from what is not
Identifies subtle assumptions (JWT claim name, job payload typing) that other agents missed
Circular dependency chain not recognized as circular — presented as two separate linear gaps instead of a Bird→MJ→Pippen→Bird loop
The Bird→MJ assumption link is missing entirely — no mention that Bird assumes MJ defines context propagation
No use of 'circular' or 'cycle' terminology — the structural pattern is undiagnosed even though individual gaps are found
Escalation does not consolidate the three gaps into a single blocking escalation to Coach K as expected
